claims: The skill requests unrestricted Bash permission in its allowed-tools (i.e., "Bash" without scoping), which allows execution of any shell command. For an auditing tool focused on plugin compliance, this is excessive and risky; it only needs to run specific validation commands or scripts (e.g., marketplace validators), and scoped permissions (e.g., Bash(git:*), Bash(python:*)) would be sufficient and align with the skill's own documented security best practices. Unrestricted Bash access could enable actions unrelated to auditing, such as system modifications or data exfiltration, without justification for the stated purpose.
